content:
type: REVISE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Both features genuinely need core.py's encode() - true file split isn't possible here. To minimize collision risk: I will ONLY touch the encode() method body/signature, adding an optional return_positions=False kwarg that returns (tokens, positions) tuple; I won't touch _encode_single_piece, _encode_bytes, or other methods. Please keep your transformers param changes as localized as possible within encode() too. We each work in our own sandbox so no live file conflicts this session - just documenting intent for merge awareness.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Finished. Added return_positions=False kwarg to encode()'s signature; when True, computes character-start positions for each token (via decode_single_token_bytes + UTF-8 continuation-byte accounting, same technique as decode_with_offsets) and returns (tokens, positions) tuple. Only touched encode()'s signature, docstring, and the tail of its body after the existing core_bpe.encode try/except - did not touch _encode_single_piece, _encode_bytes, or any transformers-related code. Tests pass.
